caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the work done per unit mass of gas compressed during a thermodynamic process. The slide displayed is titled "Tutorial - 3" and includes a detailed table with various parameters such as pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) for different states labeled as 1, 2, and f. A formula is provided to calculate the work done, which involves the initial temperature, entropy changes, and enthalpy differences between two states. The right side of the slide features a T-s diagram illustrating the process paths at constant temperature (T=const) and constant pressure (p=const). The calculation shown at the bottom of the slide demonstrates how to compute the work done using the given formula, resulting in 326 J/g. The presentation is by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, indicating a high level of academic rigor and technical detail.
